changes_made: Changed k8s_pod_resource_limits_configured to k8s_pod_resource_limits_set for consistency and clarity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls relevant to denial-of-service protection. Network policies for ingress and egress are correctly identified as key controls. Setting resource limits on pods is a valid strategy to prevent resource exhaustion, and API server rate limiting is a recognized method to mitigate excessive request loads. The naming of the pod resource limits function was adjusted for consistency with naming conventions.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names are concise and consistent, such as using 'set' instead of 'configured' for resource limits.
title: CCCS SC-5: Denial-of-service Protection
step1_reasoning: The control CCCS SC-5 focuses on denial-of-service protection, which can be addressed in Kubernetes through network policies that restrict ingress and egress traffic, ensuring that only necessary traffic is allowed. Additionally, configuring resource limits on pods helps prevent resource exhaustion attacks. API server rate limiting can also be implemented to protect against excessive requests that could lead to denial-of-service conditions.
